meaning: adj.流氓的,无赖的,卑鄙的adv.无赖地
examples: They said Kelso got some rascally adventurer, some Belgian brute, to insult his son-in-law in public.
The Welsh Captain describes Pistol as a "rascally, scald [scabby], beggarly, lousy, pragging [show-off] knave"in Henry 5.
It is almost impossible not to develop a rooting interest for the clever, rascally, Tom Sawyer.
If the economy is bad on Election Day, the rascally Republicans are to blame.
Wes Anderson, creator of the rascally stop-motion fable "Fantastic Mr. Fox", turns out to be born to make animated films.
